title: Michela Dumas
text:
Hi there! I’m Michela.



Photo of Michela petting a cow in the Swiss Alps


I’m a quantitative ecologist with a particular interest for sexual selection. I aim to answer questions related to the evolution of sexual dimorphism in the wild using long-term individual-based datasets. I’m also broadly interested in the effects of climate change and direct human impacts on wild populations.
Currently, I am Wildlife Research Analyst with the Ontario Public Service (Ministry of Natural Resources). My work contributes to monitoring caribou populations in Ontario for the purpose of management and policy decision-making
Previously, I worked as a Postdoctoral Research Fellow at the University of Ottawa, where I built on work I undertook during my PhD under the supervision of Prof. Julien Martin (M.A.D. Lab, University of Ottawa, Canada) and Dr. Pierre Bize (Bize Lab, Swiss Ornithological Institute - Vogelwarte Sempach, Switzerland).
For more information on my work, check out my projects.
I spend most of my free time backpacking or otherwise enjoying the great outdoors. You’ll find a smattering of photos from some of my favourite adventures throughout this website!

Education
 Ph.D. in Biology University of Ottawa, Ontario, Canada. 2020-2024.
 M.Sc. in Ethology Université Paris Sorbonne Nord, Ile-de-France, France. 2018-2020.
 B.Sc. in Biology Honours, with speciallization in Ecology, Ecolution, and Behaviour, with a minor in Psychology. 2012-2017.
